title: AI-SDLC SignalIngestionConfig
description:
Per-org configuration for the RFC-0030 Signal Ingestion Pipeline. Lives on disk at .ai-sdlc/signal-ingestion.yaml. The orchestrator loader (orchestrator/src/signal-ingestion/config.ts) accepts either a top-level object (legacy / minimal form) OR the canonical envelope shape with `apiVersion: ai-sdlc.io/v1alpha1`, `kind: SignalIngestionConfig`, `spec: {...}` for forward-compat with the other AI-SDLC declarative resources.

All numeric weights/thresholds MUST be non-negative finite numbers. The `enabled` flag defaults to false — the signal-ingestion pipeline is OFF until the operator explicitly opts in (gated by the `AI_SDLC_SIGNAL_INGESTION` env flag during the soak window; see `docs/operations/signal-ingestion.md` and `docs/operations/signal-ingestion-promotion.md`).

Configuration changes to this file (tier-multiplier edits, threshold tweaks, adapter list changes) are governance-relevant and emit `SignalIngestionConfigChanged` events to `events.jsonl` per RFC-0030 §11 closing note. The orchestrator computes a diff against `DEFAULT_SIGNAL_INGESTION_CONFIG` at load time so the audit trail is queryable without requiring a separate config-history store.

oneOf.title: Bare-spec form (loader-tolerated; spec fields at top level)

SignalIngestionSpec.description: Fully-resolved RFC-0030 signal ingestion spec. Every field is optional at the YAML layer — the loader fills missing fields from DEFAULT_SIGNAL_INGESTION_CONFIG. The schema documents the shape + defaults so editors with JSON Schema integration surface inline hints.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false (default), the pipeline emits no signals + the D1 composition layer falls through to the legacy backlog-only path. Flip to true after operator opt-in via the AI_SDLC_SIGNAL_INGESTION flag (soak window) or unconditionally post-promotion.

recencyHalfLifeDays.description: Exponential decay half-life in days for the recency component. Signals older than ~6 months contribute < 2% of original weight at the default. RFC-0030 §6.3.

adapters.description: Adapter names registered with the SignalSourceRegistry (e.g. signal-source-support-ticket, signal-source-community-thread, signal-source-manual). Default = ['signal-source-support-ticket', 'signal-source-community-thread']. RFC-0030 §5.

acceptedLanguages.description: BCP-47 language tags accepted by the classifier. Signals in unsupported languages are dropped + logged as a SignalLanguageUnsupported decision (RFC-0030 OQ-13.2 resolution). Default = ['en'] (v1 is English-only; multi-language deferred to v2).

TierMultipliers.description: Per-customer-tier weight multipliers applied at D1 scoring time. The Churned multiplier (default 2.0) is the highest of all tiers because demand validated by willingness-to-pay-and-found-wanting is the strongest signal of product-market gap. RFC-0030 §6.1.

IcpResonanceWeights.description: Per-ICP-resonance weight multipliers. Strong = signal source matches declared ICP segments verbatim. Partial = adjacent segment. Weak = peripheral. RFC-0030 §6.2.

Tier2SignificanceThreshold.description: Significance gate Tier 2 signals (community, competitive) must cross before they feed D1. Below threshold = monitored only. RFC-0030 §8.

minSignalCount.description: Minimum number of signals in the cluster to qualify.

minUniqueSources.description: Minimum number of distinct sources in the cluster.

minTier1SignalCount.description: Minimum number of Tier 1 signals required in the cluster ("≥1 Tier 1 signal required" is the structural defense against adversarial flooding per OQ-13.5).

minClusterAgeDays.description: Minimum cluster age in days (the cluster has persisted past initial-buzz).

SaResonanceThresholds.description: Soul Authority resonance threshold bands for D1 weighting. ≥ fullWeight → full weight. discounted ≤ x < fullWeight → weight × 0.7. excluded ≤ x < discounted → weight × 0.3 (flagged for review). x < excluded (typically 0.0) → excluded from D1. RFC-0030 §9.

ClusteringConfig.description: Clustering algorithm + threshold. BM25 + structural overlap is the default (no external dependencies). The `embedding` algorithm requires a configured RFC-0019 embedding provider adapter. RFC-0030 §7.

D1CompositionWeights.description: RFC-0030 §10 non-replacement weighting: the signal-pipeline-derived demand stream and the human-authored backlog-item demand stream both feed D1. The composer normalises the pair so any positive values are meaningful (e.g. {1, 3} → {0.25, 0.75}). Default = 50/50 — neither stream dominates out of the box. AISDLC-347 / Phase 5.
